title: let vs const
subtitle: Can the value change?
goal: Understand when to use let and when to use const.
explanation: JavaScript offers two modern ways to create variables.
const means the variable must always refer to the same value.
let means you can change the value later.
If you do not plan to change anything — use const.
If the value will change while the program runs — use let.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: const is a locked box. let is a regular box you can open and change what is inside.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Many people think const makes data completely immutable. In reality, it only prevents reassigning the variable itself.

quiz.question: Which variable should you use by default?
